industries.name: Fundamental Nuclear & Atomic Research
uses: Named after Niels Bohr. First produced at GSI by bombarding bismuth-209 with chromium-54 ions using the SHIP separator. Chemistry at PSI shows Bh forms volatile oxychlorides BhO3Cl analogous to rhenium, confirming its Group 7 position. GSI's cold fusion technique using doubly magic lead and bismuth targets revolutionized superheavy element synthesis.
Longest-lived known isotope: 270Bh, t½ = 61 seconds. First synthesized: 1981, GSI Darmstadt (Münzenberg, Hofmann group).

Research probes the limits of nuclear stability, tests relativistic quantum chemistry predictions in superheavy atoms, and searches for the theoretical island of stability predicted around Z=114, N=184.
All production requires specialized particle accelerators and isotope reactor facilities — this element cannot be made by any other means.

companies.name: GSI Helmholtz Centre
companies.use: Discovered element 107 in 1981 using the UNILAC accelerator and SHIP (Separator for Heavy-Ion Reaction Products) velocity filter — an instrument that transformed superheavy element research by allowing identification of single atoms. GSI discovered elements 107–112 using bismuth and lead targets.

companies.name: Paul Scherrer Institute (PSI)
companies.use: Performed the first gas-phase chemistry of bohrium, identifying volatile BhO3Cl consistent with rhenium chemistry. Required extraordinary precision to study just a handful of Bh atoms produced over many weeks of irradiation.

companies.name: JINR Dubna
companies.use: Conducted complementary bohrium nuclear spectroscopy experiments, contributing decay data that confirmed GSI's discovery and provided the complete nuclear decay chain used to characterize element 107.
